item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
a few lil' mistakes I noticed
<< Close

They just don’t get it; it's not about creating motion, it about creating a place where you change the constant of the universe.

This should be

They just don’t get it; it's not about creating motion, it's about creating a place where you change the constant of the universe.

Xi-5-1|Adamson: “Over and over”. “Over and over. “Over and over.”

This should be

Xi-5-1|Adamson: “Over and over”. “Over and over". “Over and over".

Xi-5-1|Adamson: “Over and over”. “Over and Over" "Something is wrong. The field is expanding. How can it be expanding?"

This should be

Xi-5-1|Adamson: “Over and over”. “Over and over". "Something is wrong. The field is expanding. How can it be expanding?"

You keep making amazing article after amazing article, and you're one of my favorite authors now. I loved the imagery in this article, which was both interesting and oddly terrifying. A definite +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks so much! I've been working on my descriptive storytelling, i.e. how much is too much. I'm glad you were able to clearly visualize the setting :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Amazing. One of the best articles I have read in a long time. Unlike other exploration logs, I was able to picture every single little detail perfectly while reading this.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cheers!

Love the mental image, but like the Capt said, they're causal loops, not temporal loops. Think of them more like those perfect loop gifs.

https://img2.thejournal.ie/inline/1317999/original/?width=500&version=1317999

https://img2.thejournal.ie/inline/1317997/original/?width=200&version=1317997

Like so.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I loved this. +1

This +1 I loved.

+1 I loved this.

Loved this +1 I.

No, seriously. This was great, and I had a mental image of video game lag the entire time I was reading it. You know, the sprite freezes mid-animation and returns back to its original position.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fantastic! Thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really love this skip, +1. Moreover, I'm doing translation of this SCP to the Chinese CN site. Will finish up in few days.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks Pasta!

Thanks Pasta!

Thanks Pasta!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was awesome! 1+

This was awesome! 1+

This was awesome! 1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for reading!

I've never experienced that personally, but googling it, it sounds terrifying. Glad this article resonated with you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the read!

My intention was each member of the Bullies became looped on something they were personally distracted by, or transfixed with.

Xi-2, who said he loved autumn, became looped when he focused on his surroundings.
Xi-4, who first focused on the bullied bike boy, became looped when focusing on her own past trauma.
Xi-3, who was studying the shut-off pattern, became looped trying to figure out if it was right.
Xi-1, who became engrossed in solving the mystery, became looped when reading the journals.

Each loop speaks to each Xi members personality in some minor way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: wow. Best ive read in a while. Really good story telling made me happy. i loved how you could never tell when someone was about to get into a loop, but somehow i think it would be better if there was a specific behavior pattern that got the bullies into a loop, but one that was hard to avoid, like, say, powerful emotion. Maybe that was already in there, but I think if it was it should be more defined.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really well done and a definite upvote.

The looping thing terrifies me on a fundamental level, and kinda reminds me of what it's like to have a bad trip from weed. (Google around for people talking about "looping" during bad trips and you'll see what I mean).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the read! I'm glad the pacing worked well. The Theta Prime connection used to be more implied than overt, but the SCP community over on reddit convinced me to add a footnote. I really like 033 as a concept, so no regrets there. Cheers!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks Crashington!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is now one of my top 10 favorite SCPs. I love the creepiness of not being able to let your guard down for even a second. Reading the logs, my heart was racing thinking about who was going next. My one complaint is the connection to Theta Prime, because I personally don’t like the concept, but that’s just me so this is still a GREAT SCP.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: loved this a lot

item.title: Re: Typolice
item.content:encoded: Fixed! Cheers.

item.title: Typolice
item.content:encoded:
In exploration log 3 you have "Site-9: Confirmed. Be care 5-3", is this suposed to be "Site-9: Confirmed. Be careful 5-3"

Still love it though

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's totally fair. Finding the balance for the observations in this exploration log was a challenge. This was a bit of a rush-out-of-the-gate, and I wish I'd gotten more crit time for it. It was quarter-end at work, didn't have much time for writing in September. Oh well, next time!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm really sorry I didn't have time to crit this. Overall I enjoyed the concept, but the execution felt a bit repetitive (lol?) for me and I wasn't a fan of the ending. Definitely some high points, but overall I'll have to neutralvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed! Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's a part in the final log where command asks for 5-1 and 5-2. I think it should just be 5-1 and 5-3. Anyways, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Any time you're free, MaliceAforethought. I'm usually in the IRC chat. Just toss me a PM. This one got rushed out the gate a bit faster than my usual articles. I'd appreciate the crit!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's a lot going on here that I like, but a lot that I don't. Poke me some time to edit in a full critique, 'cause I've got a lot to say about this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks rattles!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Another job well done, Trutherford.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Bomb?

Spoilers
Spoiled

There was no bomb. The kid didn't realize that the energy wasn't in a closed loop, it was just a machine that created a pocket-universe powered by, and powerful enough to contain the sustaining energy. You can think of it as "going back into the atoms". When the machine shut down, all that stored up energy, well- ka-BOOM! Fission.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: fixed! Thanks for spotting that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow. This is just amazing… amazing idea, nothing too over the top, just cool twist and all. I only wonder how the bomb got there? Perhaps that is for another day… +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's a spot where the italics mess up in the third log btw. really good otherwise +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks so much for the read ARD! It's always a huge help to get feedback from seasoned authors.

Establishing characterization in short-form writing projects is still a learning process for me. I'm honestly surprised; I thought I hit the mark with Xi-5, and left the core anomaly lacking (as I have in past articles). Guess there's more work to do on my story-telling element balancing act. Thanks again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks so much MightyMcB. Your continued support on this site really keeps me writing :)

I've changed the article to reflect your designation idea. I thought the flow might be halting, but honestly, the alliteration of Xi-5 makes each designation still read naturally. Thanks for the suggestion!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks LoL! Sorry, forgot to add you to the crit list. Appreciate the read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The dialogue and efforts to characterize the Mobile Task Force fall flat, but the vivid descriptions of the town and the literal banger of an ending more than make up for that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wasn't expecting that ending at all, and man it was paced beautifully. This is thoroughly excellent, T Rutherford.

Small nitpick though, I think you need a different way to differentiate between the members of Xi-5 because Xi-1 through -4 are presumably their own MTF designations. Maybe have the official transcripts call them Xi-5-1 through-4 and just have themselves refer to each other as their numbers?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fucking superb article, probably the best work I've read in the past month +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Xi-5 vs Xi squad designation was a hiccup when writing this. Xi-5 is the squad name, the team, and it sounded so good to chant. But, I also didn't want to change the team designations. Uhg. I guess I'll have to tweak it a bit.

Thanks for the read, cybersqyd, and thanks for catching that!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
god this was such a ride

+ minor thing
-minor thing

There's one case where you refer to Xi-4 as Xi-5: "Xi-3|Banks: Yeah, well, you're the Bully now, right? Xi-5, hey!"

There's also one case where it's a little ambiguous: "Xi-3|Banks: Come on Lil, snap out of it! You're not there anymore! You grew up! You're a bad-ass science commando now! You're Xi-5! Lyydia!"

I didn't just change it myself because I'm not sure whether the intention is "You're [in] Xi-5!" or "You're Xi-4!"

item.title: Entropic of Hulwick
item.content:encoded:
Don't you find it's harder when you're down?

Such an obvious reason, but nonetheless it's ours

Take the time to figure out the sound

Over and over and over and over

Far Caspian - Conversations

The third in a loosely connected article series I've been planning, born from my love of small-town America. Stay tuned!

Spoilerspoilerspoilerspoiler
Spoiled. Ka-BOOM.

A brilliant, bastard back-yard tinkerer makes a perpetual motion machine - that is, he turns the whole town into a perpetual motion machine.

Thanks to my critters: Nameless Mediocre Lord_of_Laugh Vezaz

Images used are Here and here, under Attribution-ShareAlike 2.0 Generic (CC BY-SA 2.0) and CC-BY-SA 3.0, respectively.

Thanks for reading, as always!

► More T Rutherford Articles and Tales ◄
